speaker: charlie
text: Right, science time and this one's a ripper. Researchers have figured out how to restore memory by blocking just one protein in Alzheimer's disease - PTP1B. Early trials are showing people actually getting their memories back, which honestly sounds like something out of science fiction.

speaker: alice
text: Alex, that's incredible but it gets better. Scientists in the UK are running massive clinical trials on a blood test that can detect 50 different types of cancer before you even have symptoms. One blood test, 50 cancers, potentially caught years early.

speaker: charlie
text: Mate, that's the kind of breakthrough that changes everything. And speaking of breakthroughs, physicists just achieved something called 'quadsqueezing' for the first time - a fourth-order quantum effect that could revolutionise quantum computing. No idea what that means but it sounds properly futuristic.
